query.sentence: What non-PFAS additives or formulation strategies can be used in thermoplastics to achieve low coefficient of friction (COF), good release / anti stick behavior and low surface energy?

properties: Coefficient of friction (COF) < 0.3 on typical test surfaces
Good release / anti‑stick behavior in molded or extruded parts
Thermal stability up to at least 250 °C (processing temperature)
Chemical compatibility with common thermoplastics (PP, PE, ABS, PET)
Non‑toxic and environmentally benign (no PFAS content)
constraints: Must not reduce tensile strength or modulus by more than 20 %
No phase separation or clouding in the melt
Should be processable with standard twin‑screw extruders or internal mixers
Additive must remain stable (no cross‑linking or degradation) at processing temperatures
Must not leach out during typical service conditions (≤ 100 °C)
No requirement for special safety handling beyond standard polymer additives

final_candidate.justification: Linear PDMS has a measured surface energy of ~20–22 mN/m and COF <0.2 on glass or steel when blended into thermoplastics. It is chemically inert, remains stable up to 250 °C, and does not degrade the mechanical properties of PP/PE/ABS at concentrations ≤5 wt%. Commercial grades such as Dow Corning PDMS‑1000 are readily available, non‑toxic, and inexpensive for lab use.

process_recipe.description: Dry polymer pellets (e.g., polypropylene) at 80 °C for 2 h to remove moisture.
Weigh PP and PDMS to achieve a target of 5 wt% additive. Pre‑mix in a small batch mixer.
process_recipe.equipment_class: hand mixer or small scale internal mixer

process_recipe.description: Feed the mixture into a twin‑screw extruder (lab‑scale, 50 mm screw). Set temperature profile: 200 °C in the melt zone, 180 °C in the die. Screw speed 50 rpm.
Extrude strands, cool in a water bath, and pelletize using a lab‑scale pelletizer.
Optional surface coating: spin‑coat or dip‑coat the molded part with a dilute PDMS solution (10 wt% in hexane), then cure at 80 °C for 30 min.
